steps.title: Follow the drawback
steps.content: The text says calling Agnes Grey autobiographical may make readers think Hartley merely fictionalized true events. Since creativity and inventiveness are prized in literature, that label could make readers undervalue the novel's artistic imagination.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice B shifts to identifying real-world counterparts, which the text never discusses. Choice C wrongly says Hartley claimed the novel was based on real events. Choice D invents a disagreement among critics. The needed idea is that the autobiographical label can make the novel seem less imaginative than it is. The answer is A.
